applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused ReLU+concat kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented a Triton @triton.jit kernel that fuses ReLU activation and channel concatenation. The kernel processes the output as a flat 1D array, computing (n,c,h,w) indices from flat position, branching to read from expand1x1 or expand3x3 based on channel index, applying ReLU, and storing to output. BLOCK_SIZE=512 provides optimal occupancy on MI355X.

attempted_but_failed.name: Per-(n,h,w) channel loop kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Too many kernel launches (8M+), loops inside kernel inefficient

attempted_but_failed.name: Flat concat with incorrect masking
attempted_but_failed.reason: Correctness issues with tl.where and mask logic

attempted_but_failed.name: Kernel with break statement
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton doesn't support break in loops

notes: Profiling showed 67.6% time in ReLU+concat operations
Triton kernel fuses ReLU+concat into single kernel launch
BLOCK_SIZE=512 optimal for MI355X (warp size 64, 8 warps per block)

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Agent has crashed/stalled for 3 consecutive trials (2, 3, 4) with no output, all while attempting complex 1x1 conv Triton matmul replacement. Need to take a completely different, incremental approach.

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 produced no output — 4th consecutive crash/stall. The agent keeps attempting complex Triton matmul for 1x1 convs and failing. Need to give extremely prescriptive code with simple optimizations
